name: Creta
aiSummary: The segment's default choice, the Creta pairs a wide variant spread with strong features and a panoramic-sunroof-led equipment list. The 1.5 turbo DCT adds genuine pace, but the NA petrol is the volume pick.

pros: Widest variant and powertrain spread in segment
Feature-rich cabin with ADAS on top trims
cons: NA petrol feels adequate, not quick
Top variants get expensive
Ride is soft rather than sporty

name: Seltos
aiSummary: Mechanically the Creta's twin but tuned sharper, the Seltos leans sporty with a stiffer ride and the segment's most aggressive styling. The 160 PS turbo DCT is the enthusiast pick of the class.

pros: Sharper styling and firmer handling than Creta
cons: Firm ride over broken roads
Older GNCAP score raises safety questions
Rear seat comfort trails the Creta

name: Grand Vitara
aiSummary: The Grand Vitara's strong-hybrid variants deliver hatchback-beating real-world economy in a family SUV body. Performance is modest, but ownership costs are the lowest in the class.

cons: Leisurely acceleration in all forms
Cabin plastics feel built to cost
No turbo-petrol option

name: Urban Cruiser Hyryder
aiSummary: Toyota's badge-engineered Grand Vitara adds the brand's reliability halo to the same hybrid powertrain. The strong hybrid is the reason to buy it; the mild hybrid is merely competent.

cons: Slowest 0-100 times in segment
Boot shrinks in hybrid variants
Interior shared wholesale with Grand Vitara

name: Elevate
aiSummary: The Elevate bets on Honda's refined 1.5 i-VTEC, ADAS across most trims, and class-leading ground clearance rather than gimmicks. It is honest, roomy and easy to drive — just not exciting.

pros: Honda Sensing ADAS from mid trims
cons: Single engine, no turbo or hybrid
Feature list trails Koreans at the same price
Dated infotainment graphics

name: Taigun
aiSummary: The Taigun is the driver's pick: both TSI engines are eager and the chassis is the best-balanced in the class, with a 5-star GNCAP score to match. You trade some rear-seat space and features for it.

cons: Tighter rear seat than rivals
Fewer comfort features per rupee
Higher maintenance costs

name: Kushaq
aiSummary: The Kushaq shares the Taigun's excellent TSI engines and 5-star crash rating but wraps them in slightly more conservative clothes and a marginally softer ride. The Monte Carlo trim is the visual highlight.

pros: Strong TSI powertrains shared with Taigun
cons: Three-cylinder 1.0 thrums at idle
Rear AC vents but few rear comforts
Service network thinner than rivals

name: Curvv
aiSummary: Tata's coupe-SUV brings segment-first style and a new 1.2 GDI turbo, undercutting rivals on price. Fit-finish and powertrain refinement still trail the class best.

cons: Engine refinement trails rivals
DCT calibration can hesitate
Coupe roofline cuts rear headroom

name: XUV 3XO
aiSummary: Technically sub-4-metre but cross-shopped against everything here, the 3XO packs a 5-star BNCAP score, level-2 ADAS and a strong turbo-petrol at prices the bigger SUVs cannot touch. Cabin width is its main compromise.

cons: Narrower cabin than full-size rivals
Smaller boot than segment
Brand service experience varies by city

name: Astor
aiSummary: The Astor was first to bring level-2 ADAS to the segment and still undercuts rivals on features per rupee. Ageing engines and softer resale are the trade-offs.

cons: Thirstier, older engine lineup
Weakest resale retention here
CVT dulls the NA petrol

name: Nexon
aiSummary: India's first 5-star crash-test SUV keeps its safety crown post-facelift, with a punchy 1.2 turbo and a frugal 1.5 diesel. The cabin tech leapt a generation in 2023.

pros: Petrol, diesel and EV sibling to choose from
cons: Turbo-petrol is vocal when pushed
DCA gearbox can hesitate in traffic
Rear seat tighter than Punch's upright package

name: Brezza
aiSummary: The Brezza trades outright pace for bulletproof running costs and the segment's best resale. The 1.5 NA petrol with the torque-converter auto is the relaxed pick.

cons: Modest 103 PS performance
No turbo or diesel option
Boot smaller than rivals

name: Venue
aiSummary: The Venue packs three engines and big-car features into a city-friendly footprint. The 1.0 turbo DCT is the sweet spot; the diesel is the segment's quiet overachiever.

pros: Feature list mirrors the Creta playbook
cons: 1.2 base petrol feels strained
Rear seat width is tight
Top trims overlap Creta pricing

name: Sonet
aiSummary: The Sonet is the Venue's flashier twin: same engines, sharper styling, bigger boot and more kit at the top end, including ventilated seats and a 360° camera.

pros: Diesel AT is unique at this size
cons: Firm low-speed ride
Gets pricey fully loaded
Rear bench better for two

name: Punch
aiSummary: A micro-SUV with a 5-star GNCAP score and genuine ground clearance, the Punch is the budget pick that doesn't feel like one. Just don't expect pace from the 88 PS 1.2.

cons: 88 PS means leisurely highway pace
No turbo or diesel
AMT can be jerky

name: Kiger
aiSummary: The Kiger undercuts every rival while offering a willing 1.0 turbo and a 405L boot. Cabin plastics and refinement show where the money was saved.

cons: Budget-grade cabin materials
Three-cylinder thrum ever-present
Thin dealer network

name: Magnite
aiSummary: The Magnite is the Kiger's platform twin with bolder styling and similar value-first pricing. The turbo CVT combination is genuinely pleasant in town.

cons: Interior plastics feel built to price
Smallest boot of the twins
Sparse service network

name: City
aiSummary: Three decades of refinement show: the City's 1.5 i-VTEC, huge rear seat and Honda Sensing ADAS make it the default sensible sedan. It rewards smooth drivers, not racers.

cons: No turbo or diesel option
Road noise at highway speeds
CVT drones when hustled

name: Verna
aiSummary: The Verna pairs segment-best space with a genuinely quick 160 PS turbo and a 5-star GNCAP score. The polarising styling is the only gamble.

cons: Love-or-hate styling
NA petrol is just adequate
Firm rear seat cushioning

name: Virtus
aiSummary: The Virtus is the Taigun in a sharper suit: the same excellent TSIs, 5-star rating and balanced chassis with a proper 521L boot. The GT 1.5 DSG is a junior sports sedan.

pros: 1.5 TSI GT is the enthusiast bargain
cons: Costlier servicing than Japanese rivals
Rear seat under thigh support is average
Feature list trails Koreans

name: Slavia
aiSummary: The Slavia mirrors the Virtus mechanically, trading a little flash for Skoda's slightly plusher cabin trim. Same 5 stars, same brilliant 1.5 TSI at the top.

pros: Big boot and usable rear seat
cons: Three-cylinder idle thrum on 1.0
Service network still growing
Options pricing adds up

name: Scorpio-N
aiSummary: Body-on-frame presence with surprisingly sorted road manners, the Scorpio-N's 2.2 diesel and optional 4XPLOR 4WD make it the ladder-frame default. It is big, heavy and proud of it.

pros: Imposing road presence and torque-rich diesel
cons: Heavy steering and bulk in the city
Third row is for occasional use
Petrol is thirsty

name: XUV700
aiSummary: The XUV700 brought ADAS and 200 PS to the mainstream. Monocoque comfort, seven seats and a muscular 2.2 diesel make it the do-everything family flagship.

pros: Full Level-2 ADAS on top trims
cons: Long waiting periods persist
Some cabin rattles reported
Boot small with third row up

name: Harrier
aiSummary: Land Rover-derived bones give the Harrier its planted feel; the facelift added a 5-star BNCAP score and big-screen tech. Diesel-only remains its defining choice.

cons: No petrol option at all
Kryotec diesel is gruff at idle
Patchy quality control history

name: Hector
aiSummary: The Hector sells space and screens per rupee: a limo-like rear seat, 587L boot and a 14-inch display define it. Dynamics are soft and unhurried by design.

cons: Soft, roly-poly handling
Petrol-CVT is merely adequate
Weak resale retention

name: Windsor EV
aiSummary: MG's Windsor EV is a comfort-oriented electric CUV with a massive 604L boot, lounge-like 135° reclining rear seats, and a huge 15.6-inch touchscreen. It is tailored for city commutes and spacious rear-seat comfort.

cons: No physical controls for AC and mirrors
Highway range is average on smaller pack
Soft suspension rolls in corners

name: Thar ROXX
aiSummary: The Thar ROXX is a 5-door lifestyle SUV that pairs legendary off-road capability with family-friendly comfort and a 5-star BNCAP safety rating. The mStallion petrol and mHawk diesel offer strong performance.

pros: Imposing road presence and off-road capability
cons: Heavy steering and bulk in tight spaces
Lumpy low-speed ride quality
Diesel 4x4 gets expensive at top end

name: Swift
aiSummary: The new 2024 Swift features Maruti's frugal Z12E 3-cylinder engine and standard 6 airbags. It remains the default fun-to-drive premium hatchback, balancing compact dimensions with excellent fuel efficiency.

pros: Sporty, agile handling in city traffic
cons: Engine thrums at idle under load
Boot space and cabin width are average
Gets expensive at the top end

name: Dzire
aiSummary: The first sub-4m sedan to score a 5-star GNCAP rating, the 2024 Dzire pairs premium features like an electric sunroof and 360° camera with Maruti's highly efficient Z12E engine.

pros: Frugal engine (24.8 km/l manual, 25.7 km/l AMT)
cons: 3-cylinder engine is less refined than the old 4-cylinder
Top trims cross the ₹10 Lakh mark
Rear seat width is tight for three adults

name: Amaze
aiSummary: The third-generation Honda Amaze pairs a bold 3-box design with a refined 1.2 i-VTEC engine, a smooth CVT, and standard 6 airbags. Top trims add advanced safety tech (ADAS) to the compact sedan class.

pros: Superb safety rating with standard 6 airbags
Extremely smooth i-VTEC CVT gearbox combination
Bold, mini-City design with spacious cabin
cons: Refinement at higher highway speeds could be better
No turbo or CNG options available
Ride is soft and floats over high-speed dips

name: Kylaq
aiSummary: Skoda's entry into the sub-4m SUV segment, the Kylaq features a 5-star BNCAP safety rating, a spacious boot, and a punchy 1.0 TSI engine. It brings European driving dynamics to a wider audience.

cons: Rear seat legroom is a bit tight
No diesel or CNG options
3-cylinder engine vibrates slightly at idle

name: ZS EV
aiSummary: The MG ZS EV pairs a practical 50.3 kWh battery pack with strong performance and a tech-loaded cabin. With a real-world range of ~330 km, it is a highly viable primary car for city and moderate highway usage.

cons: Bumpy ride over sharp speed breakers
Stiff competition from Curvv EV
Projected steeper resale depreciation

name: Urban Cruiser Taisor
aiSummary: Toyota's rebadged Fronx crossover, the Taisor features a coupe-SUV silhouette, a choice of 1.2L petrol or 1.0L Boosterjet turbo-petrol engines, and Toyota's trusted ownership experience.

pros: Excellent fuel economy up to 22+ km/l
Toyota service and warranty peace of mind
cons: Rear seat headroom is limited by sloping roof
Cabin plastics feel average in places
Boot space is modest at 308L

name: Innova Crysta
aiSummary: The legendary MPV continues to rule on comfort, reliability, and resale value. Driven by a robust 2.4L diesel engine, it is the default choice for long-distance family travel and fleet buyers in India.

pros: Unmatched ride comfort and spacious three rows
Bulletproof reliability and high resale value
Punchy 2.4L diesel engine with strong low-end grunt
cons: No automatic transmission option currently
Engine gets noisy at high highway speeds
Large size can be cumbersome in tight city lanes

name: Exter
aiSummary: Hyundai's micro-SUV slots below the Venue, offering a spacious cabin, a smooth 1.2L engine, standard 6 airbags, and a segment-first factory-fitted dashcam.

pros: Excellent headroom and airy cabin feel
cons: Not a true SUV — basically a raised hatchback
Looks can be polarizing
Highway overtake needs planning

name: Ioniq 5
aiSummary: Hyundai's flagship EV is a retro-futuristic masterpiece. Based on the dedicated E-GMP platform, it offers an 84 kWh battery, ultra-fast charging, a luxurious lounge-like cabin, and a real-world range of ~450 km.

pros: Dedicated EV platform yields massive cabin space
cons: Expensive price tag in India
Low ground clearance for an SUV (163mm)
No all-wheel drive option offered here

name: VF 6
aiSummary: The VinFast VF6 enters India as a premium compact electric SUV. Powered by a 59.6 kWh battery pack, it offers 174 bhp, a tech-heavy cabin, and Level 2 ADAS features as standard.

pros: Spacious cabin with clean, modern layout
cons: New brand in India with developing network
Polarizing dashboard controls
Initial price premium over petrol equivalents

name: VF 7
aiSummary: VinFast's larger EV crossover features an aggressive coupe-like silhouette, an AWD option delivering 348 bhp, a 75.3 kWh battery pack, and a premium cabin experience.

pros: Tech-laden cabin with large central screen
cons: Brand trust and service network are still unproven
Rear headroom slightly compromised by coupe roof
Steeper depreciation curve typical of new EV brands

name: Q3
aiSummary: The Audi Q3 pairs a bold design with a refined 2.0L TFSI engine, quattro AWD, and a tech-rich interior. It offers a premium, comfortable, and dynamically solid entry into luxury motoring.

pros: High-quality premium cabin and ride comfort
cons: No diesel engine option available
Interior lacks some physical control buttons
Top trims get expensive compared to space offered

name: Q5
aiSummary: A cornerstone of Audi's SUV line, the Q5 is a balanced luxury machine. Powered by a 2.0L TFSI engine putting out 249 hp, it features quattro AWD, a highly refined ride, and class-leading cabin materials.

pros: Effortless highway cruiser with a silent cabin
Quattro AWD offers strong grip in monsoons
cons: Conservative styling feels a bit dated
No diesel option in a heavy SUV segment
Underwhelming real-world fuel economy (~8-10 km/l)

name: Safari
aiSummary: Tata's flagship 3-row SUV offers imposing road presence, premium comfort, and a strong 2.0L diesel engine. With a 5-star BNCAP safety rating, it is a highly capable family highway cruiser.

pros: Spacious and luxurious 3-row cabin with captain seat option
Commanding road presence and solid build quality
Loaded with premium features like dual-zone climate and ADAS
cons: Heavy steering at slow parking speeds
Automatic transmission gets expensive
No petrol or AWD/4WD options available

name: Altroz
aiSummary: The Tata Altroz is a premium hatchback with a 5-star GNCAP rating, sporty handling, and a wide powertrain spread including a smooth dual-clutch automatic. It balances high-speed stability with robust safety.

pros: Excellent high-speed stability and ride comfort
Clean, premium design with wide-opening doors
cons: Naturally aspirated petrol engine lacks punch
Dual-clutch transmission is only on the non-turbo engine
Fit and finish levels lag behind Hyundai i20

name: Thar 3-Door
aiSummary: The legendary 3-door Thar is India's default lifestyle off-roader, featuring shift-on-the-fly 4WD, high ground clearance, and an imposing stance. RWD variants lower the entry price for urban buyers.

pros: Unmatched off-road capability and water wading
High seating position with excellent road view
Strong petrol and diesel engine options
cons: Bumpy, ladder-frame ride quality on tarmac
Tough rear seat ingress and tiny boot space
Wind noise at triple-digit speeds

name: Scorpio Classic
aiSummary: The Scorpio Classic keeps the iconic, rugged design alive with a refined 2.2L mHawk diesel engine. It's a favorite for its commanding road presence, body-on-frame durability, and robust suspension.

pros: Punchy mHawk engine with great low-end pull
Comfortable ride over broken rural roads
cons: Basic features compared to modern monocoques
Body roll in corners is pronounced
No automatic or 4WD option anymore

name: Bolero
aiSummary: The legendary Mahindra Bolero is India's quintessential utility vehicle, known for its bulletproof metal body, reliable mHawk75 engine, and unmatched ability to traverse rough rural terrain.

pros: Super reliable mechanicals and low maintenance
Excellent resale value in semi-urban areas
cons: Dated, utilitarian cabin and barebones console
Underpowered engine for fast highway runs
Lacks modern safety systems and comfort kit

name: Baleno
aiSummary: Maruti's premium hatchback is a massive seller, prioritizing cabin space, high fuel efficiency, and a smooth K-series engine. Top trims add premium features like a 360-degree camera and HUD.

pros: Frugal DualJet petrol with 22+ km/l average
cons: Build feels light compared to Altroz or i20
AMT is smooth but slower than DCT/CVT rivals
No turbo-petrol enthusiast engine available

name: Fronx
aiSummary: The Fronx is a stylish crossover that blends the Baleno's practicality with Grand Vitara-inspired SUV styling. The 1.0L BoosterJet turbo petrol offers brisk performance for enthusiast buyers.

pros: Punchy 1.0 turbo engine with torque converter AT
Frugal mileage and excellent ride comfort
cons: Rear seat headroom is slightly tight due to sloping roof
Interior design is identical to the cheaper Baleno
Rear visibility is hampered by thick C-pillars

name: Ertiga
aiSummary: The Ertiga is India's most popular family MPV, delivering unmatched space efficiency and low running costs in a compact package. Smart hybrid and factory CNG variants make it highly economical.

pros: Excellent 3-row packaging with slide and recline functions
Highly efficient petrol and CNG variants
cons: Lightweight build and average safety rating
Full load highway performance is modest
Frequent fleet usage dilutes premium personal appeal

name: Fortuner
aiSummary: The Toyota Fortuner is an iconic full-size SUV that commands unmatched prestige and resale value. Powered by a brute 2.8L diesel with 500Nm torque, it has legendary reliability and off-road capability.

pros: Brutish road presence and exceptional resale value
Legendary Toyota reliability and mechanical toughness
Extremely capable 4x4 system with low range
cons: Firm, bumpy ride quality over city roads
Gets exceptionally expensive in top trims
Interior cabin design feels dated for a ₹50 Lakh car

name: i20
aiSummary: The Hyundai i20 is a feature-rich premium hatchback offering a very spacious cabin, sharp European styling, and premium NVH levels. The smooth IVT transmission is perfect for city traffic.

pros: Striking styling and wide, comfortable cabin
Segment-first features like Bose sound system
Refined engine options and smooth automatic
cons: GNCAP rating is average at 3 stars
Frugal but unexciting base engine
Top trims are expensive

name: Tucson
aiSummary: Hyundai's flagship SUV in India, the Tucson, features futuristic styling, a premium cabin with Level 2 ADAS, and a powerhouse 2.0L diesel engine that delivers effortless highway performance.

pros: Incredibly refined and powerful 2.0L diesel engine
Spacious, luxury-car-like cabin with ADAS L2
Stunning parametric design that turns heads
cons: No fuel-efficient hybrid option in India
Firm low-speed ride on larger alloys
Premium pricing bridges close to luxury brands

name: Carens
aiSummary: The Kia Carens mixes the comfort of an MPV with the styling cues of an SUV. It is packed with features like ventilated seats, one-touch electric tumble middle seats, and standard 6 airbags.

pros: Standard safety package (6 airbags, ESC, all-wheel discs)
Refined engine options including turbo-petrol and diesel
cons: Bland-looking base profile from side view
Average 3-star safety score under older GNCAP protocol
DCT automatic gets thirsty in heavy traffic

name: Compass
aiSummary: The Jeep Compass combines premium European dynamics with rugged American heritage. Its ride and handling are class-leading, and the 2.0L Multijet diesel offers great touring capability.

pros: Superb, benchmark ride quality and handling
Solid, tank-like build and 5-star safety safety
Premium interior cabin layout and materials
cons: Slow-shifting automatic gearbox at city speeds
Tight rear-seat legroom compared to cheaper rivals
Jeep service costs can run high

name: Atto 3
aiSummary: BYD's Atto 3 is a premium electric SUV featuring their ultra-safe Blade Battery technology. With a 400+ km real-world range, quirky gym-themed interiors, and 204PS performance, it is a highly modern EV.

pros: Safe, durable Blade Battery with fast charging support
Excellent high-speed cabin insulation and ride
cons: Quirky interior details (guitar strings, round vents) polarize
Advanced driver assists are too intrusive on highways
Sparse dealer network compared to Tata/Hyundai

name: Seal
aiSummary: The BYD Seal is a premium electric sport sedan that delivers sports-car performance (530PS, AWD in top trim) and EuroNCAP safety. It is the segment benchmark for range, handling, and value.

pros: Incredible sports car acceleration (0-100 in 3.8s for AWD)
Spacious, premium cabin with rotating screen
cons: Very low ground clearance scrapes on big speed bumps
Firm suspension transmits sharp bumps in town
Rear headroom is tight for tall passengers

name: Kodiaq
aiSummary: The Skoda Kodiaq is a premium 7-seat SUV powered by a smooth 2.0L TSI petrol engine. With dynamic chassis control (DCC), solid luxury, and 4x4 traction, it offers luxury-SUV comfort at a lower price point.

pros: Dynamic Chassis Control adaptive dampers cushion the ride
Punchy 2.0L TSI petrol with smooth DSG gearbox
Superbly crafted luxury interior and smart ergonomics
cons: Third row is best left for small kids
TSI engine gets thirsty when driven aggressively
No hybrid or diesel option available
